title: Spicy Irish Pork Belly Gratin
description: This romantic dish combines the richness of pork belly with the crunch of a mirepoix-based topping and a spicy kick. Diabetic-friendly with only 35g of carbs per serving.
instructions: Season the pork belly with salt, black pepper, and allspice on all sides.
Sear the pork belly in a large skillet over medium-high heat until browned on all sides.
Transfer the pork belly to a 9x13 inch baking dish, leaving a small amount of fat behind in the skillet.
In the same skillet, sauté the chopped onion, carrot, and celery in butter until tender.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the heavy cream to the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom, and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the flour to create a roux, cooking for 1-2 minutes, until lightly browned.
Pour the cream mixture over the pork belly in the baking dish.
Sprinkle the chopped onion mixture over the top of the pork belly, followed by a sprinkle of red pepper flakes.
Bake for 1 hour, or until the top is golden brown and the internal temperature of the pork belly reaches 160°F (71°C).
Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Reheat any leftovers in the oven until hot and bubbly, about 10 minutes at 350°F (180°C).
Note: This dish can be made in advance and refrigerated or frozen for later use, simply thaw and reheat as needed.
